MTH6139 Time Series

Coursework 1 - Forecasting the GBP/USD Exchange Rate with Prophet

Mohammad Ruhan-Ur Rahman

Spring Term 2025

Introduction

This project analyses the GBP/USD exchange rate using Meta’s Prophet model. The aim is to identify trends and produce a short-term forecast.

Data and setup

The data is daily GBP/USD exchange rates downloaded from Yahoo Finance.

library(quantmod)
library(prophet)
library(ggplot2)

getSymbols("GBPUSD=X", src = "yahoo", from = "2020-01-01")
## [1] "GBPUSD=X"
gbp_usd_data <- data.frame(
  ds = as.Date(index(`GBPUSD=X`)),
  y = as.numeric(Cl(`GBPUSD=X`))
)

Exploratory analysis

ggplot(gbp_usd_data, aes(x = ds, y = y)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "GBP/USD Exchange Rate", x = "Date", y = "Rate")

The series shows high volatility and no clear seasonal pattern.

summary(lm(y ~ ds, data = gbp_usd_data))
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = y ~ ds, data = gbp_usd_data)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -0.21938 -0.04312 -0.00098  0.05269  0.12909 
## 
## Coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  1.350e+00  4.649e-02  29.037   <2e-16 ***
## ds          -3.001e-06  2.396e-06  -1.253    0.211    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.06313 on 1616 degrees of freedom
##   (6 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.0009701,  Adjusted R-squared:  0.0003519 
## F-statistic: 1.569 on 1 and 1616 DF,  p-value: 0.2105

A simple trend suggests the exchange rate changes over time but is unstable.

Prophet model

model <- prophet(gbp_usd_data)

future <- make_future_dataframe(model, periods = 30)

forecast <- predict(model, future)
plot(model, forecast)

The forecast shows short-term movement with wide uncertainty.

prophet_plot_components(model, forecast)
## Warning: `aes_string()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.0.0.
## ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`.
## ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the prophet package.
##   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/facebook/prophet/issues>.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

Trend is the main feature, with weak seasonality.

Alternative model

model2 <- prophet(gbp_usd_data,
                  weekly.seasonality = FALSE,
                  yearly.seasonality = FALSE)

forecast2 <- predict(model2, make_future_dataframe(model2, periods = 30))
plot(model2, forecast2)

Removing seasonality does not change results much.

Discussion

The GBP/USD series is more irregular than standard textbook time series. This means forecasts should be interpreted carefully, especially during periods of market uncertainty.

Conclusion

GBP/USD shows volatility and weak seasonality. Prophet works well for visualising trends but forecasts should be interpreted carefully.

References

  • Prophet documentation: https://facebook.github.io/prophet/docs/quick_start.html#r-api
  • Yahoo Finance: https://finance.yahoo.com
